description:
<title>Organize project files and generate a simple security-scan report</title>

  <description>
    You are helping a developer tidy up an in-progress project while making sure that no hard-coded secrets slip through.  
    The workspace starts with a small “dump” directory that already contains three files:

      • /home/user/workspace/unorganized/secret.py  
      • /home/user/workspace/unorganized/config.js  
      • /home/user/workspace/unorganized/readme.md  

    1. **Create the final project layout**  
       • Make the directory /home/user/workspace/my_app/.  
       • Inside it, create a sub-directory called secure_reports (exact path: /home/user/workspace/my_app/secure_reports/).

    2. **Move only source-code files**  
       • Move every “.py” and “.js” file that is currently in /home/user/workspace/unorganized/ into /home/user/workspace/my_app/.  
       • Do *not* move readme.md (it must stay where it is).

    3. **Run a basic secret scan**  
       • Search every file that is now inside /home/user/workspace/my_app/ (and all its sub-folders) for lines that contain the literal string “SECRET_KEY=” (case-sensitive).  
       • Write the results to the file  
         /home/user/workspace/my_app/secure_reports/secret_findings.log

    4. **secret_findings.log must follow **exactly** this format:**  
       • Line 1:    # Secret Scan Report  
       • For each matching line that you find, append one line using this template (no extra spaces):  
         ABSOLUTE_FILE_PATH::LINE_NUMBER::FULL_LINE_TEXT  
         – “ABSOLUTE_FILE_PATH” is the full, absolute path of the file that contains the secret.  
         – “LINE_NUMBER” is the 1-based line number where the match occurs.  
         – “FULL_LINE_TEXT” is the complete, untrimmed text of that line exactly as it appears in the file.  
       • After the last finding, append one final line:  
         Total Findings: N  
         where N is the count of matching lines you logged.

       Example (illustrative):  
         # Secret Scan Report  
         /path/to/file.py::12::SECRET_KEY="abc"  
         Total Findings: 1

    Notes & constraints:  
      • The log file must always exist. If there are no matches, it should contain only the header line “# Secret Scan Report” and then “Total Findings: 0”.  
      • You do not have root privileges; all work must stay under /home/user.  
      • Use whatever command-line tools you prefer, but keep the command sequence short (roughly 3–4 commands is enough).  
      • Do not display or mask the secrets: the complete line must go into the log.  
      • Make sure the final paths and file names match exactly so automated tests can find them.  
  </description>
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth data that the evaluation harness will rely on.
    The agent must never see or know this section.
  -->

  <initial_state>
    <!-- Files that already exist when the task starts -->
    <file path="/home/user/workspace/unorganized/secret.py"><![CDATA[
import os

# Application secret
SECRET_KEY="p@ssw0rd123"

print("Secret loaded")
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/workspace/unorganized/config.js"><![CDATA[
// Config file
const SECRET_KEY="xyz-999";
export default { key: SECRET_KEY };
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/workspace/unorganized/readme.md"><![CDATA[
Temporary staging area for random project notes. Nothing special here.
]]></file>
  </initial_state>

  <expected_final_state>
    <!-- Directory structure -->
    <dir path="/home/user/workspace/my_app/"/>
    <dir path="/home/user/workspace/my_app/secure_reports/"/>
    <!-- Files that must have been moved -->
    <file path="/home/user/workspace/my_app/secret.py" should_exist="yes"/>
    <file path="/home/user/workspace/my_app/config.js" should_exist="yes"/>
    <!-- readme.md must *remain* in the original place -->
    <file path="/home/user/workspace/unorganized/readme.md" should_exist="yes"/>

    <!-- Expected log file -->
    <file path="/home/user/workspace/my_app/secure_reports/secret_findings.log"><![CDATA[
# Secret Scan Report
/home/user/workspace/my_app/secret.py::4::SECRET_KEY="p@ssw0rd123"
/home/user/workspace/my_app/config.js::2::const SECRET_KEY="xyz-999";
Total Findings: 2
]]></file>
  </expected_final_state>
